source: Ash 2026-05-14 — wants live-updating visualization of Pi's memory after L4 deprecation
title: Per-fact L2 vault sync with auto-linked entities for Foam graph view
what_failed: After deprecating L4 (T-079), Ash still wanted visible proof Pi was learning. The existing sync_l2_to_vault wrote ONE markdown file per category (all preferences in preferences.md). Foam's graph view treats each file as a node, so the whole memory store was 5-10 nodes max — useless as visualization.
where_failed: tools/tools_obsidian.py::sync_l2_to_vault aggregated facts by category instead of emitting one file per fact.
why_likely: Vault sync was originally designed for human reading (consolidated per-category lists), not for graph visualization (one node per fact).
suggested_fix: Add sync_l2_facts_to_vault() that emits one markdown file per L2 fact under vault/notes/memory/<category>/<slug>.md with YAML frontmatter, [[category]] back-link, and Title-Case entity auto-linkification (_linkify_entities). Generate per-category index files at vault/notes/memory/<category>.md listing all facts as [[slug]] links. Generate top-level vault/notes/memory/index.md. Hook into sync_vault() so it runs at session exit alongside the other sync steps. Foam graph view then shows: fact nodes, category hub nodes, entity hub nodes (Title-Case auto-linked).
